extraction.one_line_summary: KWIATKOWSKI Michal won the 6.6km Valence prologue by 1.66 seconds over van Emden, exploiting Sky's multi-leader ambiguity while Thomas crashed.
tactical_patterns: Sky entered with Thomas as designated leader but Kwiatkowski's prologue win creates deliberate leadership ambiguity for mountain stages.
GC favorites Bardet and Nibali lost 50+ seconds in 6.6km prologue, signaling poor preparation one month pre-Tour.
Van Emden's 7:26 benchmark eliminated major climbers early, proving even short flat prologues expose Tour preparation deficits.

rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: positive: Won after 40-day racing hiatus, suggesting Sky's delayed race calendar optimizes Tour form over early-season racing.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Crashed midway through 6.6km prologue, losing 21 seconds, indicating either recon failure or physical vulnerability pre-peak.
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: Lost 50+ seconds in flat prologue despite two consecutive Tour podiums, revealing time trial weakness persists.
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: Finished ~50th at +50 seconds after three-year Dauphiné absence, suggesting race unfamiliarity compromises even proven champions.
rider_signals.VAN EMDEN Jos: positive: Set early benchmark 7:26 that eliminated multiple GC contenders, proving Lotto's TT specialists undervalued in stage race prologues.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: Lost 21 seconds but matched Thomas's deficit, confirming expected TT limitations without collapse.
